devanagari: ब्रह्मोवाच । ज्ञातोऽसि मेऽद्य सुचिरान्ननु देहभाजां । न ज्ञायते भगवतो गतिरित्यवद्यम् । नान्यत्त्वदस्ति भगवन्नपि तन्न शुद्धं । मायागुणव्यति...
english_devnagari: brahmovāca jñāto na jñāyate bhagavato gatirityavadyam nānyattvadasti bhagavannapi tanna śuddhaṃ māyāguṇavyatikarād yadururvibhāsi

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[nominative single from √ jñā class 3 verb], [nominative single from √ jñā class 9 verb]
[accusative single from √ śudh class 1 verb], [accusative single from √ śudh class 4 verb]
[nominative single from √ śudh class 1 verb], [accusative single from √ śudh class 1 verb], [nominative single from √ śudh class 4 verb], [accusative single from √ śudh class 4 verb]

translation:
Brahmā said:
Oh Lord! It is after a penance for a very long time that you have been (luckily) realized by me today. It is indeed the defect of beings conditioned by body that your real essential nature is not understood by them. Nothing other than you, exists. (Anything else that appears to be) is not pure (is false) because it is you who appear to be many due to the intermixture of the guṇas of Māyā.[1]
purport:
Footnote [1]:
According to Bhāgavata Candrikā: This verse expresses tḥe self satisfaction of Brahmā’s God-realization, viz. the whole of this universe consisting of cit and acit is the body of God, and it explains that there is nothing which is outside Brahman.
Oh Lord! Really it is after the acquisition (accumulation) of penance for a pretty long period that you have been now seen by me in your real form. The Kṣetrajñas (individual Souls) conditioned by physical body as a result of their karmas, do not know your real nature which fully possesses the six attributes of Bhagavān. Oh Lord, there is no such thing as is other than you. (You are its soul When you are realised everything is realized). Gods and other things which appear independent of (other than) you are not the objects of correct perception, due to the effect of the attribute of Māyā or Prakṛti. Though you are one, you shine (manifest yourself) as many.
nanu... avadyam etc.—Padaratnāvalī explains: That the real nature of the Lord is not realized by beings is erroneous, as the Lord, though invisible in form, is realized by devotion. It is due to sin that God is not realized by beings. Not that there is non-existence of things other than the Lord, but they do not exist independently, but owe their existence to him. But things other than you are impure, and you are absolutely pure. You are present by your incarnations in mahat and other principles. It is due to the guṇas of Prakṛti that things other than you are defective.
Kramasandarbha.: Though you are pure in your internal capacity of infinite glory, you appear many in the form of the world as a result of the intermixture of the attributes of your external power called Māyā.

devanagari: रूपं यदेतदवबोधरसोदयेन । शश्वन्निवृत्ततमसः सदनुग्रहाय । आदौ गृहीतमवतारशतैकबीजं । यन्नाभिपद्मभवनाद् अहमाविरासम् ॥ २ ॥
english_devnagari: rūpaṃ yadetadavabodharasodayena śaśvannivṛttatamasaḥ sadanugrahāya ādau gṛhītamavatāraśataikabījaṃ yannābhipadmabhavanād ahamāvirāsam

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ grah class 9 verb], [accusative single from √ grah class 9 verb]
[nominative single from √ i class 2 verb], [vocative single from √ i class 2 verb]

translation: This is your form from which, due to eternal manifestation of the power of intelligence, ignorance or tamas disappears,—form which, from the beginning, you have assumed for conferring (your) grace on the good; and which is the seed (source) of hundreds of incarnations. And from His region of the navel-lotus, I came into existence (lit. was manifested).

devanagari: नातः परं परम यद्‍भवतः स्वरूपम् । आनन्दमात्रमविकल्पमविद्धवर्चः । पश्यामि विश्वसृजमेकमविश्वमात्मन् । भूतेन्द्रियात्मकमदस्त उपाश्रितोऽस्मि ॥...
english_devnagari: nātaḥ paraṃ parama yad‍bhavataḥ svarūpam ānandamātramavikalpamaviddhavarcaḥ paśyāmi viśvasṛjamekamaviśvamātman bhūtendriyātmakamadasta upāśrito
translation: Most excellent Lord! I do not see any form of yours other than this beautiful one of manifest light, beyond (i.e. destitute of) differences, and full of bliss. Hence I resort to this one form of yours which creates the world but itself is different from it, and which is the source of the bhūtas (elements) and sense-organs[2].
purport:
Footnote [2]:
Oh Supreme One, I do not see your essential form other than this form which is full of bliss (dispelling all miseries), devoid of the differences of jātis and guṇas, whose knowledge (or will—saṅkalpas) is unobstructed; which is different from the universe, yet it is the cause consisting of bhūtas and sense organs. I take shelter of this form—Bhāgavata Candrikā

devanagari: तद्वा इदं भुवनमङ्गल मङ्गलाय । ध्याने स्म नो दर्शितं त उपासकानाम् । तस्मै नमो भगवतेऽनुविधेम तुभ्यं । योऽनादृतो नरकभाग्भिरसत्प्रसङ्गैः ॥ ४ ...
english_devnagari: tadvā idaṃ bhuvanamaṅgala maṅgalāya dhyāne sma no darśitaṃ ta upāsakānām tasmai namo bhagavate yo

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ dṛś ], [accusative single from √ dṛś ]

translation: Oh auspiciousness incarnate (i.e. God who is auspicious to the universe), you have really manifested this very (form) to your devotees in their meditation for their good (or prosperity), we offer obeisance to you (a gracious) Lord who is spurned by the atheists (like Mīmāṃsakas, Sāṅkhyas etc.) and believers in false logic and who therefore deserve (stay in) Hell.

devanagari: ये तु त्वदीयचरणाम्बुजकोशगन्धं । जिघ्रन्ति कर्णविवरैः श्रुतिवातनीतम् । भक्त्या गृहीतचरणः परया च तेषां । नापैषि नाथ हृदयाम्बुरुहात्स्वपुंसा...
english_devnagari: ye tu tvadīyacaraṇāmbujakośagandhaṃ jighranti karṇavivaraiḥ śrutivātanītam bhaktyā gṛhītacaraṇaḥ parayā ca teṣāṃ nāpaiṣi nātha hṛdayāmburuhātsvapuṃsām

[nominative single from √ nī class 1 verb], [accusative single from √ nī class 1 verb]

translation: Oh Lord, you do not go away from the lotus-like hearts of your men (devotees) who enjoy by the passage of their ears the fragrance of your īotus-bud-Iike feet which is carried (to them) by wind (in the form) of the Vedas and who clasped your feet with intense (and unswerving) devotion.

devanagari: तावद्‍भयं द्रविणगेहसुहृन्निमित्तं । शोकः स्पृहा परिभवो विपुलश्च लोभः । तावन्ममेत्यसदवग्रह आर्तिमूलं । यावन्न तेऽङ्घ्रिमभयं प्रवृणीत लोकः ...
english_devnagari: tāvad‍bhayaṃ draviṇagehasuhṛnnimittaṃ śokaḥ spṛhā paribhavo vipulaśca lobhaḥ tāvanmametyasadavagraha ārtimūlaṃ yāvanna te

translation: So long as the people do not resort to your feet which offer freedom from fear of saṃsāra, they face the fear pertaining to wealth, house and friends (and caused by) sorrow, desire, dishonour, covetousness and false sense of attachment to possessions (lit. ‘this is mine’) which is the cause of sorrow.

devanagari: दैवेन ते हतधियो भवतः प्रसङ्गात् । सर्वाशुभोपशमनाद् विमुखेन्द्रिया ये । कुर्वन्ति कामसुखलेशलवाय दीना । लोभाभिभूतमनसोऽकुशलानि शश्वत् ॥ ७ ॥
english_devnagari: daivena te hatadhiyo bhavataḥ prasaṅgāt sarvāśubhopaśamanād vimukhendriyā ye kurvanti kāmasukhaleśalavāya dīnā lobhābhibhūtamanaso

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative plural from √ kṛ class 8 verb], [vocative plural from √ kṛ class 8 verb], [accusative plural from √ kṛ class 8 verb]

translation: Those are verily deprived of their senses by their fate, whose senses (minds) are averse to (listening and eulogising) your deeds which remove all inauspiciousness (misery etc.). Those wretched ones, with minds overpowered with, avarice, continuously perform misdeeds for a very slight pleasure for gratifying their low desires.

devanagari: क्षुत्तृट्त्रधातुभिरिमा मुहुरर्द्यमानाः । शीतोष्णवातवर्षैरितरेतराच्च । कामाग्निनाच्युत रुषा च सुदुर्भरेण । सम्पश्यतो मन उरुक्रम सीदते मे ...
english_devnagari: kṣuttṛṭtradhātubhirimā muhurardyamānāḥ śītoṣṇavātavarṣairitaretarācca kāmāgninācyuta ruṣā ca sudurbhareṇa sampaśyato mana urukrama sīdate me

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [accusative plural from √ śā class 4 verb], [ablative single from √ śā class 4 verb], [genitive single from √ śā class 4 verb]
[ablative single from √ śā class 4 verb], [genitive single from √ śā class 4 verb]

translation: Oh Acyuta, Lord of wide strides (as Trivikrama), my heart is deeply pained to see these (people) constantly afflicted by hunger, thirst and the disturbance of three bodily humours (viz. kapha, pitta and vāta), by heat and cold, by wind and rain and by other (ādhibhautika) factors and by unbearable fire of desires and anger.

devanagari: यावत् पृथक्त्वमिदमात्मन इन्द्रियार्थ । मायाबलं भगवतो जन ईश पश्येत् । तावन्न संसृतिरसौ प्रतिसङ्क्रमेत । व्यर्थापि दुःखनिवहं वहती क्रियार्थ...
english_devnagari: yāvat pṛthaktvamidamātmana indriyārtha māyābalaṃ bhagavato jana īśa paśyet tāvanna saṃsṛtirasau pratisaṅkrameta vyarthāpi duḥkhanivahaṃ vahatī kriyārthā

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative dual from √ vah class 1 verb], [vocative dual from √ vah class 1 verb], [accusative dual from √ vah class 1 verb]

translation: Oh Lord, while (so long as) people will see (regard) this body (and other things) as different from the Soul (or God), due to the dominant influence of the Lord’s Māyā, appearing as the organs of senses and their objects, this worldly existence (saṃsāra), unreal as it is, will not disappear, but will bring a host of troubles (and miseries) as a result of karmas.

devanagari: अह्न्यापृतार्तकरणा निशि निःशयाना । नानामनोरथधिया क्षणभग्ननिद्राः । दैवाहतार्थरचना ऋषयोऽपि देव । युष्मत् प्रसङ्गविमुखा इह संसरन्ति ॥ १० ॥
english_devnagari: ahnyāpṛtārtakaraṇā niśi niḥśayānā nānāmanorathadhiyā kṣaṇabhagnanidrāḥ daivāhatārtharacanā ṛṣayo yuṣmat prasaṅgavimukhā iha saṃsaranti

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [imperfect active second plural], [imperfect middle third single]
[nominative plural from √ sṛ class 1 verb], [vocative plural from √ sṛ class 1 verb], [accusative plural from √ sṛ class 1 verb]

translation: Oh God, even sages revolve in the course of worldly existence (saṃsāra) in this world, if they are averse to eulogizing your deeds (and such forms of devotion). They who by day, have their senses absorbed in doing actions (for getting worldly objects), and go to sleep with minds full of different desires, get their sleep disturbed every moment, and their endeavours after their objects are frustrated by Fate.

devanagari: त्वं भक्तियोगपरिभावितहृत्सरोज । आस्से श्रुतेक्षितपथो ननु नाथ पुंसाम् । यद् यद् धिया ते उरुगाय विभावयन्ति । तत्तद् वपुः प्रणयसे सदनुग्रहा...
english_devnagari: tvaṃ bhaktiyogaparibhāvitahṛtsaroja āsse śrutekṣitapatho nanu nātha puṃsām yad yad dhiyā te urugāya vibhāvayanti tattad vapuḥ praṇayase sadanugrahāya

[nominative single from √ śru class 5 verb], [nominative dual from √ śru class 5 verb], [vocative single from √ śru class 5 verb], [vocative dual from √ śru class 5 verb], [accusative dual from √ śru class 5 verb]
[nominative single from √ i class 2 verb], [vocative single from √ i class 2 verb], [accusative single from √ i class 2 verb]
[nominative single from √ i class 2 verb], [vocative single from √ i class 2 verb], [accusative single from √ i class 2 verb]

translation: Oh Lord, you, path to whom is perceived by the (type of devotion called) śravaṇa (listening to the glories of the Lord)[3], really dwell in the lotus-like hearts of men (devotees) purified by Bhakti-yoga. Oh Lord of unbounded glory, in order to confer your grace on the devotees, you manifest yourself in whatever form they meditate upon you.
purport:
Footnote [3]:
Bhāgavata Candrikā takes śruta as ‘knowledge derived from Śāstras and īkṣita as ‘knowledge derived from deep thought about what is permanent and what is perishable’.
Padaratnāvalī takes īkṣita as ‘knowledge derived from meditation and interprets, ‘Those to whom the path of devotion to Viṣṇu is shown by study of śāstras under a pious and devout preceptor.’
VC.: The path which was first heard (learnt) from the preceptor, and then realized by meditation.

devanagari: नातिप्रसीदति तथोपचितोपचारैः । आराधितः सुरगणैर्हृदि बद्धकामैः । यत्सर्वभूतदययासदलभ्ययैको । नानाजनेष्ववहितः सुहृदन्तरात्मा ॥ १२ ॥
english_devnagari: nātiprasīdati tathopacitopacāraiḥ ārādhitaḥ suragaṇairhṛdi baddhakāmaiḥ yatsarvabhūtadayayāsadalabhyayaiko nānājaneṣvavahitaḥ suhṛdantarātmā
translation: [4] The Lords dwells in all things. He is the only friend and ruler from within. He becomes extremely pleased by compassion to all beings which is impossible to be found in non-devotees. He is not so much pleased even though he is propitiated with rich articles of worship, (even) by gods who cherish some desires in their hearts. (God is easily attainable only to desireless devotees).
purport:
Footnote [4]:
Bhāgavata Candrikā: You are not so much pleased (so as to favour the bliss of Liberation) with your devotees (suragaṇaiḥ) who cherish desire into their hearts and propitiate you with various articles (etc.) of worship, as you are pleased by that type of unselñsh compassion, which is based on the knowledge that ill feeling towards any being is ill feeling against you—a compassion impossible to be found in people who identify body with the Soul (asad-alabhyaya). Though present within all, you are not affected by their defects, as you support and control them as an antarātmā.
Bālaprabodhini: Just as God becomes extremely pleased by compassion to all beings (without any ulterior selñsh motive), he is not pleased by the rich worship even of gods who cherish some desire in their hearts, i.e. though the Lord grants the desired objects to his sakāma devotees, he does not bless them with self-realization. The Lord being present in all, the ill feeling of sakāma devotees against some persons is an ill feeling against the Lord whom they try to propitiate. Hence he is not much pleased with them as with desireless devotees.

devanagari: पुंसामतो विविधकर्मभिरध्वराद्यैः । दानेन चोग्रतपसा परिचर्यया च । आराधनं भगवतस्तव सत्क्रियार्थो । धर्मोऽर्पितः कर्हिचिद् ध्रियते न यत्र ॥ ...
english_devnagari: puṃsāmato vividhakarmabhiradhvarādyaiḥ dānena cogratapasā paricaryayā ca ārādhanaṃ bhagavatastava satkriyārtho dharmo
translation: The righteous acts which are offered to you never perish. Hence your propitiation, Oh Lord, is the best fruit of all religious acts, such as various righteous deeds, performance of sacrifices, religious gifts, austere penance and observance of religious vows.

devanagari: शश्वत्स्वरूपमहसैव निपीतभेद । मोहाय बोधधिषणाय नमः परस्मै । विश्वोद्‍भवस्थितिलयेषु निमित्तलीला । रासाय ते नम इदं चकृमेश्वराय ॥ १४ ॥
english_devnagari: śaśvatsvarūpamahasaiva nipītabheda mohāya bodhadhiṣaṇāya namaḥ parasmai viśvod‍bhavasthitilayeṣu nimittalīlā rāsāya te nama idaṃ cakṛmeśvarāya
translation: Salutations to (you) the Supreme Lord, who are eternally free from the illusion of difference,[5] by your own essential light of intelligence, and whose vidyā-śakti is knowledge itself[6]. We offer our homage to you, the controller (of the universe), whose sports and diversion are the Māyā, the cause of the origin, sustenance and destruction of the universe.
purport:
Footnote [5]:
bheda-moha: Bhāvārtha-dīpikā-prakāśa enumerates these misapprehensions—
(1) the notion of the Lord being imperfect: (2) Some other deity, other than Viṣṇu being the Supreme Ruler; (3) the distinctions (between the levels of) araiāras; (4) The notion that the individual Soul is the Supreme Being. (5) That the Supreme Soul is not the individual Soul. (6) That both the Supreme Soul and the individual Soul are distinct.
Bhāgavata Candrikā takes bheda as the difference in the categories of jīva, e.g. man, God and moha as the ignorance caused by the misapprehension of the identity of the Soul and the body.

Footnote [6]:
VC. (i) Whose enjoyment (rasa) is the sportive looking at Māyā the cause of the origination etc. of the universe.
VC.: (ii) Oh cause of the creation etc. of the universe, whose sport is the special dance with gopīs known as rāsa.

devanagari: यस्यावतार गुणकर्मविडम्बनानि । नामानि येऽसुविगमे विवशा गृणन्ति । तेऽनैकजन्मशमलं सहसैव हित्वा । संयान्त्यपावृतामृतं तमजं प्रपद्ये ॥ १५ ॥
english_devnagari: yasyāvatāra guṇakarmaviḍambanāni nāmāni ye te saṃyāntyapāvṛtāmṛtaṃ tamajaṃ prapadye

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [present middle first single], [injunctive middle first single]

translation: I resort to that unborn Being by uttering whose names signifying his incarnations (e.g. Devakī-nandana—‘Devaki’s son’), his attributes (e.g. sarvajña ‘Omniscient’), deeds (eg. kaṃsāri ‘Enemy or killer of Kaṃsa) (even) in an unconscious stage, at the time of death, people at once become free from the sins of many births, and attain to Brahman uncovered by (the veil of) Māyā.

devanagari: यो वा अहं च गिरिशश्च विभुः स्वयं च । स्थित्युद्‍भवप्रलयहेतव आत्ममूलम् । भित्त्वा त्रिपाद्ववृध एक उरुप्ररोहः । तस्मै नमो भगवते भुवनद्रुमा...
english_devnagari: yo vā ahaṃ ca giriśaśca vibhuḥ svayaṃ ca sthityud‍bhavapralayahetava ātmamūlam bhittvā tripādvavṛdha eka uruprarohaḥ tasmai namo bhagavate bhuvanadrumāya
translation: Salutations to the Lord who is the universe-Tree which is the only one, but has three feet (branches) viz. Brahmā, I (Viṣṇu) and Śiva, each of which has numerous branches (e.g. the seven sages etc.)—the tree which has its roots in the Supreme Soul and has differentiated itself by three attributes (guṇas under the names of Brahmā, Viṣṇu and Rudra) who are the cause of the creation, sustenance and destruction of the universe.

devanagari: लोको विकर्मनिरतः कुशले प्रमत्तः । कर्मण्ययं त्वदुदिते भवदर्चने स्वे । यस्तावदस्य बलवान् इह जीविताशां । सद्यश्छिनत्त्यनिमिषाय नमोऽस्तु तस...
english_devnagari: loko vikarmanirataḥ kuśale pramattaḥ karmaṇyayaṃ tvadudite bhavadarcane sve yastāvadasya balavān iha jīvitāśāṃ sadyaśchinattyanimiṣāya namo

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative dual from √ vad class 1 verb], [vocative dual from √ vad class 1 verb], [accusative dual from √ vad class 1 verb], [locative single from √ vad class 1 verb]

[nominative dual from √ yas class 4 verb], [vocative dual from √ yas class 4 verb], [accusative dual from √ yas class 4 verb]

translation: Salutation to the all-powerful, ever-vigilant (unwinking god in the form of) Time who instantaneously cuts asunder the hope of life of this world as long as the people shirk[7] the performance of your worship which is conducive to their own good, and which has been directly ordained by you, and are engaged in the prohibited path of Karma.
purport:
Footnote [7]:
Bhāgavata Candrikā reads an avagraha (i.e. a-) before vikarma-nirata [nirataḥ] and pramatta [pramattaḥ] and explains: Time severs shortly the attachment to saṃsāra of a man who is very careful in the performance of auspicious and sacred duties of propitiating God according to his caste (varṇa) and stage of life (āśrama)—duties prescribed by you in the Vedas—and who does not indulge in impious deed.

devanagari: यस्माद्‍बिभेम्यहमपि द्विपरार्धधिष्ण्यं । अध्यासितः सकललोकनमस्कृतं यत् । तेपे तपो बहुसवोऽवरुरुत्समानः । तस्मै नमो भगवतेऽधिमखाय तुभ्यम् ॥ ...
english_devnagari: yasmād‍bibhemyahamapi dviparārdhadhiṣṇyaṃ adhyāsitaḥ sakalalokanamaskṛtaṃ yat tepe tapo bahusavo tasmai namo bhagavate

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ i class 2 verb], [vocative single from √ i class 2 verb], [accusative single from √ i class 2 verb]
[perfect middle first single], [perfect middle third single]
[perfect middle first single], [perfect middle third single]

translation: Even though I have occupied a place (viz. Brahmāhood in Satya-Ioka) which lasts for a period of two parārdha years and which is bowed down by all the worlds, I am afraid of him (your form called Time). Hence with a desire to attain you, I practised penance for many years and performed many sacrifices. My salutations to you, the Almighty Lord of sacrifices.[8]
purport:
Footnote [8]:
Siddhāntapradīpa construes differently: I bow to the presiding deity of sacrifices of whom I am afraid, though I occupy the post (Bahmāhooḍ) lasting for two parārdha years. With a desire to attain Vaikuṇṭha (your residence) which is honoured (bowed to) by all people, I performed penance for many long years.

devanagari: तिर्यङ्मनुष्यविबुधादिषु जीवयोनि । ष्वात्मेच्छयात्मकृतसेतुपरीप्सया यः । रेमे निरस्तविषयोऽप्यवरुद्धदेहः । तस्मै नमो भगवते पुरुषोत्तमाय ॥ १...
english_devnagari: tiryaṅmanuṣyavibudhādiṣu jīvayoni ṣvātmecchayātmakṛtasetuparīpsayā yaḥ reme nirastaviṣayo tasmai namo bhagavate puruṣottamāya

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect middle first single], [perfect middle third single]

translation: Salutations to Lord Puruṣottama who, with a view to observing the laws ordained by him, assumed of his own accord the bodies (avatāras or incarnations) in the various forms of living beings, such as sub-human beings (e.g. birds, beasts), human beings, gods etc. and enjoyed himself, though he is (absolutely) unattached to worldly pleasures.

devanagari: योऽविद्ययानुपहतोऽपि दशार्धवृत्त्या । निद्रामुवाह जठरीकृतलोकयात्रः । अन्तर्जलेऽहिकशिपुस्पर्शानुकूलां । भीमोर्मिमालिनि जनस्य सुखं विवृण्वन...
english_devnagari: yo nidrāmuvāha jaṭharīkṛtalokayātraḥ antarjale bhīmormimālini janasya sukhaṃ vivṛṇvan

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation: Though he is never affected by the five varieties of Avidyā[9], he, who has conserved the universe in his belly with a view to increasing the pleasure of rest (for people who are exhausted by their activities in the previous kalpa—period, assumed on the waters, tossed with terrible waves, yogic sleep, for which the touch of the body of the Serpent Śeṣa was favourable.
purport:
Footnote [9]:
Bhāgavata Purāṇa III. 12.2 enumerates the following vṛttis (powers) of Avidyā: viz. mahā-moha, moha, tāmisra, andha-tāmisra and tamas. As Pātañjala Yoga Pradīpa (Pātañjala-Yoga-Pradīpa [Pātañjalayogapradīpa]) points out, the hindrances (kleśas) in YS. II.3, viz. avidyā (= tamas), asmitā (=moha) rāga (=mahāmoha), dveṣa (=tāmisra) and abhiniveśa (=andhatāmisra) are known in the Sāṅkhya system by the names given in the brackets above (Classical Sāṃkhya. p. 295).
Īśvara Kṛṣṇa further classifies them as follows: “There are eight varieties of obscurity (tamas) and delusion (moha); ten kinds of extreme delusion (mahā-moha); both gloom (tāmisra) and utter darkness (andha-tāmisra) are 18 fold. G.J. Larson’s Classical Sāṃkhya, p. 275.

devanagari: यन्नाभिपद्मभवनाद् अहमासमीड्य । लोकत्रयोपकरणो यदनुग्रहेण । तस्मै नमस्त उदरस्थभवाय योग । निद्रावसानविकसन् नलिनेक्षणाय ॥ २१ ॥
english_devnagari: yannābhipadmabhavanād ahamāsamīḍya lokatrayopakaraṇo yadanugraheṇa tasmai namasta udarasthabhavāya yoga nidrāvasānavikasan nalinekṣaṇāya

translation: Oh Praiseworthy Lord, I, who came into being in the lotus-mansion of your navel, and who became, through your favour, an instrument for the creation of the three worlds, bow to you in whose stomach lies the whole world, and who has opened his lotus-like eyes at the completion of your yogic sleep.

devanagari: सोऽयं समस्तजगतां सुहृदेक आत्मा । सत्त्वेन यन्मृडयते भगवान् भगेन । तेनैव मे दृशमनुस्पृशताद्यथाहं । स्रक्ष्यामि पूर्ववदिदं प्रणतप्रियोऽसौ ...
english_devnagari: so sattvena yanmṛḍayate bhagavān bhagena tenaiva me dṛśamanuspṛśatādyathāhaṃ srakṣyāmi pūrvavadidaṃ praṇatapriyo

[nominative single from √ i class 2 verb], [vocative single from √ i class 2 verb], [accusative single from √ i class 2 verb]

translation: May the Lord furnish my intelligence with that knowledge and omnipotence with which he gladdens the world, so that I may be able to create this (universe) as it was in the previous kalpa—the Lord who is the friend of all the worlds, and dwells within them as the antaryāmin, is gracious (dear) to his devotees.

devanagari: एष प्रपन्नवरदो रमयात्मशक्त्या । यद्यत् करिष्यति गृहीतगुणावतारः । तस्मिन्स्वविक्रममिदं सृजतोऽपि चेतो । युञ्जीत कर्मशमलं च यथा विजह्याम् ॥...
english_devnagari: eṣa prapannavarado ramayātmaśaktyā yadyat kariṣyati gṛhītaguṇāvatāraḥ tasminsvavikramamidaṃ sṛjato yuñjīta karmaśamalaṃ ca yathā vijahyām

[ablative single from √ sṛj class 6 verb], [genitive single from √ sṛj class 6 verb]

translation: This (Lord) grants boons to those (devotees) who resort to him. He takes incarnations retaining (his original) qualities (like omnipotence, omniscience), along with his own (or soul-) power called Lakṣmī (and not his Māyā power). May he direct my mind to whatever he does while I create, by his order, this universe which is also his own glory, so that I can renounce my attachment to actions, and the consequent sin.

devanagari: नाभिह्रदादिह सतोऽम्भसि यस्य पुंसो । विज्ञानशक्तिरहमासमनन्तशक्तेः । रूपं विचित्रमिदमस्य विवृण्वतो मे । मा रीरिषीष्ट निगमस्य गिरां विसर्गः...
english_devnagari: nābhihradādiha sato vijñānaśaktirahamāsamanantaśakteḥ rūpaṃ vicitramidamasya vivṛṇvato me mā rīriṣīṣṭa nigamasya girāṃ visargaḥ
translation: I am born here as the vijñāna-śakti[10] (the presiding deity over citta or the principle called mahat) from the deep lakelike navel of the Man of infinite powers, lying on the (cosmic) waters. May there be no loss of the utterance[11] of the Vedas, while I am detailing the wonderful form of this Man viz. the universe.
purport:
Footnote [10]:
vijñāna-śakti—(i) The competence to utter the Veda—(Bhāvārtha-dīpikā-prakāśa) (ii) my knowledge-form—(Bhāgavata Candrikā) (iii) my knowledge of Vedas—(Padaratnāvalī) (iv) myself being the vijñāna; my being the presiding deity of the principle of intelligence (VC.).

Footnote [11]:
visarga—(i) Contact of teaching and studying (the Vedas)—Bhāvārtha-dīpikā-prakāśa
(ii) utterance—Bhāvāratha Dīpikā, Bhāgavata Candrikā, Bhāvāratha Dīpikā explains that as per the famous adage “The plough is the end of Vedic lore”, Brahmā was afraid that he would be out of touch with the Vedas while engaged in creation. Hence this prayer to retain his Vedic lore.
(iii) The definite conclusion (viz. Viṣṇu is the most supreme).—Padaratnāvalī

devanagari: सोऽसौ अदभ्रकरुणो भगवान् विवृद्ध । प्रेमस्मितेन नयनाम्बुरुहं विजृम्भन् । उत्थाय विश्वविजयाय च नो विषादं । माध्व्या गिरापनयतात्पुरुषः पुरा...
english_devnagari: so premasmitena nayanāmburuhaṃ vijṛmbhan utthāya viśvavijayāya ca no viṣādaṃ mādhvyā girāpanayatātpuruṣaḥ purāṇaḥ
translation: May this Lord, the most Primitive Man, of infinite grace, get up, opening his lotus-like eyes, and with profusely affectionate smiles. May he remove our dejection by his sweet words for the successful creation of the universe.

devanagari: मैत्रेय उवाच । स्वसम्भवं निशाम्यैवं तपोविद्यासमाधिभिः । यावन्मनोवचः स्तुत्वा विरराम स खिन्नवत् ॥ २६ ॥
english_devnagari: maitreya uvāca svasambhavaṃ niśāmyaivaṃ tapovidyāsamādhibhiḥ yāvanmanovacaḥ stutvā virarāma sa khinnavat

translation:
Maitreya said:
Thus having seen his own creator by his power of penance, knowledge and meditation, and having praised him to the best of the abilities of his mind and speech, the god Brahmā remained silent as if exhausted.

devanagari: अथाभिप्रेतमन्वीक्ष्य ब्रह्मणो मधुसूदनः । विषण्णचेतसं तेन कल्पव्यतिकराम्भसा ॥ २७ ॥
english_devnagari: athābhipretamanvīkṣya brahmaṇo madhusūdanaḥ viṣaṇṇacetasaṃ tena kalpavyatikarāmbhasā
translation: Then noting the difficulty of Brahmā who got nervous at (the lack of) his knowledge regarding (prospective) construction of the universe, and whose mind was dejected at the sight of the tumultuous deluvian waters, Madhusūdana (Viṣṇu) spoke to him in deep emphatic voice, as if to remove his dejection (lit. sin).

devanagari: लोकसंस्थानविज्ञान आत्मनः परिखिद्यतः । तमाहागाधया वाचा कश्मलं शमयन्निव ॥ २८ ॥
english_devnagari: lokasaṃsthānavijñāna ātmanaḥ parikhidyataḥ tamāhāgādhayā vācā kaśmalaṃ śamayanniva

translation: Then noting the difficulty of Brahmā who got nervous at (the lack of) his knowledge regarding (prospective) construction of the universe, and whose mind was dejected at the sight of the tumultuous deluvian waters, Madhusūdana (Viṣṇu) spoke to him in deep emphatic voice, as if to remove his dejection (lit. sin).

devanagari: श्रीभगवानुवाच । मा वेदगर्भ गास्तन्द्रीं सर्ग उद्यममावह । तन्मयाऽपादितं ह्यग्रे यन्मां प्रार्थयते भवान् ॥ २९ ॥
english_devnagari: śrībhagavānuvāca mā vedagarbha gāstandrīṃ sarga udyamamāvaha tanmayā

[nominative dual from √ yam class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ yam class 1 verb], [vocative dual from √ yam class 1 verb], [accusative dual from √ yam class 1 verb]

translation:
The Lord said:
Oh Vedagarbha (i.e. Brahmin), do not get lax (due to despair). Exert yourself for the creation (of the universe'). What you pray of me, has been already obtained for you.

devanagari: भूयस्त्वं तप आतिष्ठ विद्यां चैव मदाश्रयाम् । ताभ्यां अन्तर्हृदि ब्रह्मन् लोकान् द्रक्ष्यसि अपावृतान् ॥ ३० ॥
english_devnagari: bhūyastvaṃ tapa ātiṣṭha vidyāṃ caiva madāśrayām tābhyāṃ antarhṛdi brahman lokān drakṣyasi apāvṛtān
translation: Oh Brahman, perform penance again and practise the (yogic) lore of concentrating on me. You will find in your heart, the unfolding of (the plan of the creation of) the worlds, by both of these (tapas and samādhi).

devanagari: तत आत्मनि लोके च भक्तियुक्तः समाहितः । द्रष्टासि मां ततं ब्रह्मन् मयि लोकान् त्वमात्मनः ॥ ३१ ॥
english_devnagari: tata ātmani loke ca bhaktiyuktaḥ samāhitaḥ draṣṭāsi māṃ tataṃ brahman mayi lokān tvamātmanaḥ

[nominative single from √ tan class 8 verb], [accusative single from √ tan class 8 verb]

translation: Oh Brahman, when you are full of devotion and properly poised in meditation, you will see me pervading you and the world, and yourself and the world reposing in me.

devanagari: यदा तु सर्वभूतेषु दारुष्वग्निमिव स्थितम् । प्रतिचक्षीत मां लोको जह्यात्तर्ह्येव कश्मलम् ॥ ३२ ॥
english_devnagari: yadā tu sarvabhūteṣu dāruṣvagnimiva sthitam praticakṣīta māṃ loko jahyāttarhyeva kaśmalam

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ sthā class 1 verb], [accusative single from √ sthā class 1 verb]

translation: When the people will realize me as dwelling within all beings like fire dormant in the wood, they will immediately shed off sins or misery.

devanagari: यदा रहितमात्मानं भूतेन्द्रियगुणाशयैः । स्वरूपेण मयोपेतं पश्यन् स्वाराज्यमृच्छति ॥ ३३ ॥
english_devnagari: yadā rahitamātmānaṃ bhūtendriyaguṇāśayaiḥ svarūpeṇa mayopetaṃ paśyan svārājyamṛcchati

[nominative single from √ rah class 1 verb], [accusative single from √ rah class 1 verb], [nominative single from √ rah class 10 verb], [accusative single from √ rah class 10 verb]

translation: When a person sees that his Self is free from bhūtas (elements), indriyas (sense-organs), guṇas (attributes) and antaḥkaraṇa (the mind), and is essentially one with me, he attains identification with Brahman.

devanagari: नानाकर्मवितानेन प्रजा बह्वीः सिसृक्षतः । नात्मावसीदत्यस्मिन् ते वर्षीयान् मदनुग्रहः ॥ ३४ ॥
english_devnagari: nānākarmavitānena prajā bahvīḥ sisṛkṣataḥ nātmāvasīdatyasmin te varṣīyān madanugrahaḥ

translation: It is my great blessing that your mind will never get despaired of this creation of the universe, while you desire to create innumerable subjects with various details of actions.

devanagari: ऋषिमाद्यं न बध्नाति पापीयान् त्वां रजोगुणः । यन्मनो मयि निर्बद्धं प्रजाः संसृजतोऽपि ते ॥ ३५ ॥
english_devnagari: ṛṣimādyaṃ na badhnāti pāpīyān tvāṃ rajoguṇaḥ yanmano mayi nirbaddhaṃ prajāḥ saṃsṛjato

[ablative single from √ sṛj class 6 verb], [genitive single from √ sṛj class 6 verb]

translation: As your mind is firmly fixed on me, the evil attribute of rajas shall not bind you, the first-born sage, even while you indulge in procreating the beings.

devanagari: ज्ञातोऽहं भवता त्वद्य दुर्विज्ञेयोऽपि देहिनाम् । यन्मां त्वं मन्यसेऽयुक्तं भूतेन्द्रियगुणात्मभिः ॥ ३६ ॥
english_devnagari: jñāto yanmāṃ tvaṃ manyase

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ jñā class 3 verb], [nominative single from √ jñā class 9 verb]
[nominative single from √ i class 2 verb], [vocative single from √ i class 2 verb]
[nominative single from √ i class 2 verb], [vocative single from √ i class 2 verb], [accusative single from √ i class 2 verb]
[present middle second single], [present passive second single]

translation: Inasmuch as you realize me as unconnected with bhūtas, sense-organs, attributes and egohood (ahaṃkāra), I have been known by you today, though I am very difficult to be known by corporate beings (or beings attached to body).

devanagari: तुभ्यं मद्विचिकित्सायां आत्मा मे दर्शितोऽबहिः । नालेन सलिले मूलं पुष्करस्य विचिन्वतः ॥ ३७ ॥
english_devnagari: tubhyaṃ madvicikitsāyāṃ ātmā me darśito nālena salile mūlaṃ puṣkarasya vicinvataḥ
translation: When through the lotus-stalk you tried to find out the root of the lotus under water, and when a doubt as to my existence arose in your mind, my true self was revealed to you within you.

devanagari: यच्चकर्थाङ्ग मत्स्तोत्रं मत्कथा अभ्युदयांकितम् । यद्वा तपसि ते निष्ठा स एष मदनुग्रहः ॥ ३८ ॥
english_devnagari: yaccakarthāṅga matstotraṃ matkathā abhyudayāṃkitam yadvā tapasi te niṣṭhā sa eṣa madanugrahaḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ i class 2 verb], [vocative single from √ i class 2 verb], [accusative single from √ i class 2 verb]
[imperative active second single], [perfect active first single], [perfect active second plural], [perfect active third single]
[nominative single from √ mad class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ mad class 1 verb]

translation: It was indeed my grace that you made the prayer composed of my glorious deeds, or that you had firm adherence to penance.

devanagari: प्रीतोऽहमस्तु भद्रं ते लोकानां विजयेच्छया । यद् अस्तौषीर्गुणमयं निर्गुणं मानुवर्णयन् ॥ ३९ ॥
english_devnagari: prīto yad astauṣīrguṇamayaṃ nirguṇaṃ mānuvarṇayan

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ prī class 4 verb], [nominative single from √ prī class 9 verb]

translation: I am pleased with you. May you be prosperous, as you, desiring success (in the creation) of worlds, have praised my describing me as attributeless (though I appear to be full of attributes.

devanagari: य एतेन पुमान्नित्यं स्तुत्वा स्तोत्रेण मां भजेत् । तस्याशु सम्प्रसीदेयं सर्वकामवरेश्वरः ॥ ४० ॥
english_devnagari: ya etena pumānnityaṃ stutvā stotreṇa māṃ bhajet tasyāśu samprasīdeyaṃ sarvakāmavareśvaraḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ i class 2 verb], [accusative single from √ i class 2 verb]

translation: I, who am the Supreme God, the bestower of all desired boons, will immediately be pleased with a person who will always worship me, and pray to me with this prayer (which you have composed).

devanagari: पूर्तेन तपसा यज्ञैः दानैर्योगसमाधिना । राद्धं निःश्रेयसं पुंसां मत्प्रीतिः तत्त्वविन्मतम् ॥ ४१ ॥
english_devnagari: pūrtena tapasā yajñaiḥ dānairyogasamādhinā rāddhaṃ niḥśreyasaṃ puṃsāṃ matprītiḥ tattvavinmatam

[nominative single from √ man class 4 verb], [accusative single from √ man class 4 verb], [nominative single from √ man class 8 verb], [accusative single from √ man class 8 verb]

translation: It is the considered opinion of the knowers of the Reality, that my grace is the summum bonum to be achieved by pūrta (acts for public welfare), penance, sacrifices, gifts and yogic meditation.

devanagari: अहमात्मात्मनां धातः प्रेष्ठः सन् प्रेयसामपि । अतो मयि रतिं कुर्याद् देहादिर्यत्कृते प्रियः ॥ ४२ ॥
english_devnagari: ahamātmātmanāṃ dhātaḥ preṣṭhaḥ san preyasāmapi ato mayi ratiṃ kuryād dehādiryatkṛte priyaḥ
translation: Oh Brahmā, I am the (Supreme) Soul of all beings, the most beloved of all the beloved objects. Hence one should concentrate his love in me, as body and its other belongings are loved for my sake.

devanagari: सर्ववेदमयेनेदं आत्मनाऽत्माऽत्मयोनिना । प्रजाः सृज यथापूर्वं याश्च मय्यनुशेरते ॥ ४३ ॥
english_devnagari: sarvavedamayenedaṃ ātmanā prajāḥ sṛja yathāpūrvaṃ yāśca mayyanuśerate
translation: By means of yourself who is the Veda incarnate, and is created by me, you create again as before (in the previous kalpa the beings that are lying within me.

devanagari: मैत्रेय उवाच । तस्मा एवं जगत्स्रष्ट्रे प्रधानपुरुषेश्वरः । व्यज्येदं स्वेन रूपेण कञ्जनाभस्तिरोदधे ॥ ४४ ॥
english_devnagari: maitreya uvāca tasmā evaṃ jagatsraṣṭre pradhānapuruṣeśvaraḥ vyajyedaṃ svena rūpeṇa kañjanābhastirodadhe

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation:
Maitreya said:
Having explained this (the process of creating the universe) to the creator of the world (Brahmā), the Controller of Prakṛti and Puruṣa, with lotus-like navel disappeared in his own form (as Nārāyaṇa).
